enonce.texte: Match the words with their correct definitions.

questions.reponse: To clean something with water and soap.

sousQuestions.reponse: To clean something with water and soap.

questions.reponse: A substance used for washing and cleaning.

sousQuestions.reponse: A substance used for washing and cleaning.

questions.reponse: Very small living things that can cause diseases.

sousQuestions.reponse: Very small living things that can cause diseases.

intitule: Using the Simple Imperative for Hygiene

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct imperative form of the verb in brackets.

intitule: Reading and Understanding a Text About Hygiene

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions.

Keeping Our Environment Clean

Hygiene is very important for our health. We must wash our hands with soap and water before eating. We must also dispose of garbage properly. Germs and bacteria can cause diseases if we are not careful. We should clean our homes, schools, and surroundings every day. We should also cover our food to protect it from flies and dust. Remember: cleanliness is next to godliness.

questions.texte: Why is hygiene important according to the text?
questions.reponse: Hygiene is important for our health.

questions.texte: What should we do before eating?
questions.reponse: We should wash our hands with soap and water before eating.

questions.texte: What can germs and bacteria cause?
questions.reponse: Germs and bacteria can cause diseases.

questions.texte: What does the text say we should cover our food?
questions.reponse: We should cover our food to protect it from flies and dust.

enonce.texte: Create a short slogan (2-3 sentences) or a poster idea to promote hygiene and sanitation at your school.

questions.texte: Write a short slogan or poster idea for hygiene at school.
questions.reponse: Example: 'Wash your hands, stay healthy! Keep our school clean and safe.'

sousQuestions.texte: Create a slogan using imperative forms.
sousQuestions.reponse: Wash your hands, stay healthy! Keep our school clean and safe.

enonce.texte: Write a short dialogue between two friends discussing the importance of hygiene and sanitation.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue of about 6 to 8 exchanges.
questions.reponse: Example: A: Hi, B. Have you washed your hands? B: Yes, I have. We must always wash our hands. A: That's true. Germs can make us sick. B: I also think we should keep our classroom clean. A: Yes, we should dispose of trash in the bin. B: And we should clean our desks every day. A: Good idea! Cleanliness is important for our health.

sousQuestions.texte: Write a dialogue with at least 6 exchanges.
Use at least three hygiene vocabulary words.

enonce.texte: Create a poster for your school about hygiene and sanitation. Include a title, 3-4 rules, and a slogan. Write the text of your poster below.
images: A school poster about hygiene and sanitation with rules and a slogan.

questions.texte: Write the text for your poster.
questions.reponse: Example: Title: HYGIENE AT SCHOOL. Rules: 1. Always wash your hands with soap. 2. Dispose of garbage in the bins. 3. Keep your classroom clean. 4. Do not leave food uncovered. Slogan: 'Stay clean, stay healthy!'

sousQuestions.texte: Write a title, 3-4 rules, and a slogan.
sousQuestions.reponse: Title: HYGIENE AT SCHOOL. Rules: 1. Always wash your hands with soap. 2. Dispose of garbage in the bins. 3. Keep your classroom clean. 4. Do not leave food uncovered. Slogan: 'Stay clean, stay healthy!'
